title: Building from git repo with dockerfile does not work
body:
**Description**

I want to build a container by passing an URL pointing to a git repository containing a dockerfile file.
This works fine with docker, but buildah is not able to find a 'FROM' statement -> "no FROM statement found"

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. buildah build-using-dockerfile https://github.com/carroarmato0/Flasko.git
2. podman build https://github.com/carroarmato0/Flasko.git

The repository contains the following content:

```
[carroarmato0@neon-flower:~] $ tree Flasko/
Flasko/
├── Dockerfile
├── LICENSE
├── README.md
└── src
    ├── flasko.py
    └── requirements.txt
```

```
[carroarmato0@neon-flower:~] $ head Flasko/Dockerfile 
# Base container
FROM centos:7

# Administration
LABEL maintainer="carroarmato0@gmail.com"

# Allow changing the Subject of the greeting inside the application at runtime
ENV HELLO_SUBJECT="World"
...
```

**Describe the results you received:**
```
no FROM statement found
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

I would expect the git repository to be temporarily cloned locally and used as context

**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**

```
buildah/bionic,now 1.9.0-1~dev~ubuntu18.04~ppa13 amd64 [installed]
buildah/bionic 1.9.0-1~dev~ubuntu18.04~ppa2 i386
```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
Version:         1.9.0-dev
Go Version:      go1.10.4
Image Spec:      1.0.0
Runtime Spec:    1.0.0
CNI Spec:        0.4.0
libcni Version:  
Git Commit:      
Built:           Fri May 24 01:35:41 2019
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman version` if reporting a `podman build` issue:**

```
Version:            1.3.2-dev
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.10.4
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `cat /etc/*release`:**

```
DISTRIB_ID=Ubuntu
DISTRIB_RELEASE=19.04
DISTRIB_CODENAME=disco
DISTRIB_DESCRIPTION="Ubuntu 19.04"
NAME="Ubuntu"
VERSION="19.04 (Disco Dingo)"
ID=ubuntu
ID_LIKE=debian
PRETTY_NAME="Ubuntu 19.04"
VERSION_ID="19.04"
HOME_URL="https://www.ubuntu.com/"
SUPPORT_URL="https://help.ubuntu.com/"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/"
PRIVACY_POLICY_URL="https://www.ubuntu.com/legal/terms-and-policies/privacy-policy"
VERSION_CODENAME=disco
UBUNTU_CODENAME=disco
```

**Output of `uname -a`:**

```
Linux neon-flower 5.0.0-15-generic #16-Ubuntu SMP Mon May 6 17:41:33 UTC 2019 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

**Output of `cat /etc/containers/storage.conf`:**

```
# The "container storage" table contains all of the server options.
[storage]

# Default Storage Driver
driver = "overlay"

# Temporary storage location
runroot = "/var/run/containers/storage"

# Primary read-write location of container storage
graphroot = "/var/lib/containers/storage"

[storage.options]
# AdditionalImageStores is used to pass paths to additional read-only image stores
# Must be comma separated list.
additionalimagestores = [
]

# Size is used to set a maximum size of the container image.  Only supported by
# certain container storage drivers (currently overlay, zfs, vfs, btrfs)
size = ""

# OverrideKernelCheck tells the driver to ignore kernel checks based on kernel version
override_kernel_check = "true"
```

**Output of `cat ~/.config/containers/storage.conf`:**

```
[storage]
  driver = "overlay"
  runroot = "/tmp/1000"
  graphroot = "/home/carroarmato0/.local/share/containers/storage"
  [storage.options]
    size = ""
    remap-uids = ""
    remap-gids = ""
    remap-user = ""
    remap-group = ""
    ostree_repo = ""
    skip_mount_home = ""
    mount_program = "/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs"
    mountopt = ""
    [storage.options.thinpool]
      autoextend_percent = ""
      autoextend_threshold = ""
      basesize = ""
      blocksize = ""
      directlvm_device = ""
      directlvm_device_force = ""
      fs = ""
      log_level = ""
      min_free_space = ""
      mkfsarg = ""
      mountopt = ""
      use_deferred_deletion = ""
      use_deferred_removal = ""
      xfs_nospace_max_retries = ""

```

comments.body: Hi @carroarmato0, thanks for opening the issue. I can confirm that there's an issue with git repositories. We'll have a look!

> Hi @carroarmato0, thanks for opening the issue. I can confirm that there's an issue with git repositories. We'll have a look!

Thanks, cloning a repository and cd'ing in the directory for building works as a workaround
It seems that buildah is mistakenly downloading the html file instead of the raw one.
